title: Asus rolls out a ROG 20th anniversary chair and backpack, alongside commemorative components and peripherals ROG Destrier Edition 20, ROG SLASH Hard-case Luggage Edition 20 are back in black (and gold)
summary: Asus celebrates its ROG 20th anniversary with special edition products, including a gaming chair and luggage.
insight: Premium branding extension through commemorative lifestyle accessories

title: Seven hospitalized after toxic gas fire at SK hynix advanced memory plant Cheongju 4th campus incident today led to all 3,600 staff being evacuated
summary: A fire broke out at SK hynix's Cheongju 4th campus, releasing toxic gas. All 3,600 staff were evacuated, with seven hospitalized due to exposure. The incident occurred in a room where fluorine gas was used.
insight: Mass evacuation highlights risks in high-tech manufacturing facilities.

title: Bambu Lab Launches Big Bed Slinger: A2L company's 'H2S Lite' is half the cost of H2S at just $469
summary: Bambu Lab has launched a new product called Big Bed Slinger, priced at $469, which is half the cost of its previous 'H2S' model. The 'H2S Lite' is a more affordable option for users. The combo package is available for $569.
insight: Cost-effective technology option for users with limited budgets

title: 256GB of dual-channel RAM hits mass market thanks to Origin Code quad-rank CUDIMM packs 128GB of DDR5-8000 into a single module
summary: Origin Code unveiled 256GB dual-channel RAM kits at Computex 2025. The Vortex 4R DDR5-8000 CUDIMM packs 128GB into a single module. This represents a significant upgrade in memory capacity.
insight: Mass market access to high-capacity dual-channel RAM modules.

title: Tom's Hardware Unfiltered: Computex 2026, Day 0 peek behind the curtain to see how we're covering the biggest trade show of the year
summary: Our team is covering Computex 2026 from the ground up, sharing behind-the-scenes content and documenting the challenges of reporting from the massive trade show. This year, we're giving our readers an unprecedented look at the process of covering a major tech event, from setup and planning to the finished articles. From interviews with industry experts to hands-on time with the latest products, we'll be sharing it all.
insight: Unfiltered reporting showcases the authenticity of tech journalism experiences.

title: Nvidia says RTX Spark chip will support all major anti-cheat and DRM technologies Fortnite, Valorant, Denuvo, and more to work natively with Windows on Arm
summary: Nvidia's RTX Spark chip will support major anti-cheat and DRM technologies, making multiplayer games more accessible on Windows on Arm devices, with Fortnite being the first game to run natively with Easy Anti-Cheat.
insight: Widespread anti-cheat support boosts gaming on Windows on Arm devices

title: Asus' monstrous ROG Astral GeForce RTX 5090 Edition 20 includes expansive curved AMOLED display also debuts 3,000W power supply and striking PC case
summary: Asus has unveiled the ROG Astral GeForce RTX 5090 Edition 20, featuring a 20-inch curved AMOLED display, a 3,000W power supply, and a striking PC case. This commemorative gaming gear celebrates Asus' ROG gaming sub-brand. The system showcases cutting-edge technology and design.
insight: Asus pushes gaming PC innovation with extreme specs and design.

title: Supermicro shows off Vera Rubin NVL72 rack with all-new type of coolant company claims coolant offers 1,000 times higher electrical impedance over standard cooling
summary: Supermicro showcases servers using AMD EPYC Venice, MI450, and Nvidia Vera Rubin solutions.
insight: Revolutionary coolant boosts server electrical impedance by 1,000 times.

title: Save 78% on Nord's latest Complete VPN package 27 months of online protection for $107
summary: Get 27 months of NordVPN coverage for $107, a significant discount. This package includes fast VPN connections, anti-virus protection, and a password manager. The monthly cost works out to $3.99.
insight: Long-term VPN protection at an affordable, discounted rate.

title: US closes loophole that allowed Chinese-owned subsidiaries located outside China to buy AI chips report claims that hundreds of thousands of advanced AI chips have been acquired through BIS blind spot
summary: The US has closed a loophole allowing Chinese subsidiaries to acquire AI chips. A clarification from the BIS now includes foreign-based Chinese subsidiaries in US export controls. Hundreds of thousands of AI chips are estimated to have been acquired through this loophole.
insight: US export control loophole allowed massive AI chip acquisition by China.

title: Negative time experiment clears peer review as photons appear to leave an atom cloud before entering groundbreaking quantum 'negative time' proven after 1 million test runs
summary: A University of Toronto experiment has published findings in Physical Review Letters, demonstrating that photons can spend a negative amount of time inside a cloud of atoms after 1 million test runs. This groundbreaking 'negative time' phenomenon challenges traditional notions of time and space in quantum mechanics.
insight: Quantum mechanics defies traditional time concepts, enabling negative time experiences.

title: Acer s PM131QT portable monitor is a 12.3-inch touchscreen with magnetic mounting, a built-in kickstand, and 5-point touch 1920 x 720 IPS screen has pogo pins for a keyboard, and is designed for secondary and in-vehicle use
summary: Acer's PM131QT is a compact 12.3-inch touchscreen monitor for versatile use. It features magnetic mounting, a built-in kickstand, and 5-point touch IPS screen. Suitable for secondary and in-vehicle use.
insight: Portable monitor enhances productivity for on-the-go multitasking experiences.

title: Resourceful runner 'can race my own ghost' using homemade Meta Ray-Ban Display app also adds bonus coins, mini leaderboard, and more
summary: A resourceful runner created a custom app for Meta Ray-Ban Display, allowing for gamified running experiences with features like racing against personal bests and earning bonus coins.
insight: Gamification enhances running engagement and motivation through interactive technology.

title: Computex 2026 Live: Every update and announcement from day one in Taipei
summary: Follow Computex 2026 live updates from Taipei, covering major announcements and reveals.
insight: Tech industry trends and innovations set for global adoption soon after.

title: AMD promises 13% uplift with new EXPO Ultra Low Latency overclocking on DDR5 DIMMs automatic memory overclocking delivers 4% improvement over standard EXPO, says AMD
summary: AMD's EXPO Ultra Low Latency overclocking promises 13% improvement over DDR5 speeds and 4% over standard EXPO. This new technology enables automatic memory overclocking for enhanced performance. The benefits extend to improved gaming and content creation experiences.
insight: Enhanced gaming and content creation performance through optimized memory speeds

title: Intel warns it has 'a healthy dose of paranoia' over Nvidia entrance into PC market company says RTX Spark is 'great for the market' while touting the virtues of x86
summary: Intel expresses skepticism towards Nvidia's entrance into consumer SoCs with RTX Spark, emphasizing x86 benefits.
insight: Intel defends x86 architecture against potential Nvidia SoC threat.

title: We went hands-on with Qualcomm's new '$300 and up' ARM laptop platform with mystery eight-core CPU active-cooled Snapdragon C laptop surfaces in Acer Aspire Go 15
summary: Qualcomm's Snapdragon C laptop platform has been showcased at Computex 2026, featuring an active-cooled eight-core CPU. The platform is designed for laptops priced from $300. Details were revealed through demos of Windows utilities on a demo unit.
insight: First ARM-based laptops could disrupt budget laptop market dynamics.

title: DLSS 4.5 Ray Reconstruction update arrives in August for better ray tracing visuals broader training data set and second-gen transformer architecture combine for improved image quality
summary: Nvidia's DLSS 4.5 Ray Reconstruction offers improved image quality with a broader training data set and second-gen transformer architecture.
insight: Enhanced ray tracing visuals for a more immersive gaming experience.

title: Microsoft Surface Laptop Ultra weilds Nvidia's RTX Spark superchip with 128GB of RAM, 20 Arm CPU cores, and a Blackwell GPU 15-inch mini-LED PixelSense Ultra display rounds out the powerful package
summary: The Surface Laptop Ultra is powered by Nvidia's RTX Spark Superchip, featuring 20 Arm CPU cores and 6,144 CUDA cores, along with up to 128GB of unified memory.
insight: Unprecedented performance in a portable laptop with AI-accelerated capabilities integrated.

title: Nvidia lays out RTX Spark roadmap for laptops and desktop PCs at Computex 2026 three generations outlined, Rubin with LPDDR6 memory, followed by Rosa Feynman
summary: Nvidia reveals three generations of RTX Spark platforms for laptops and desktops, starting with Rubin and LPDDR6 memory, followed by Rosa Feynman.
insight: Future-proofing PC gaming and AI applications with consistent hardware upgrades.

title: Nvidia unveils RTX Spark Superchip for laptops and desktop PCs at Computex 2026 new platform promises to turn Windows into an agentic AI OS with Arm CPU, Blackwell GPU, and 128GB unified memory
summary: Nvidia introduced the RTX Spark Superchip at Computex 2026, a platform that combines an Arm CPU, Blackwell GPU, and 128 GB of unified memory to power agentic AI on Windows.
insight: Revolutionizing AI on Windows with integrated, high-performance hardware capabilities.

title: Intel Xeon 7 Diamond Rapids CPUs officially launching in 2027 on Intel 18A-P next-gen P-core Xeon features PCIe 6.0, 50% higher core counts, and twice the memory bandwidth
summary: Intel Xeon 7 Diamond Rapids CPUs launch in 2027, featuring 50% higher core counts and twice the memory bandwidth. This next-gen Xeon aims to compete against AMD's EPYC Venice CPUs. The 18A-P process and PCIe 6.0 support are notable upgrades.
insight: Intel boosts performance and competitiveness with Xeon 7 Diamond Rapids update.

title: Intel details long-awaited Crescent Island AI GPU at Computex, boasts up to 480 GB of LPDDR5X to combat memory shortages company shares more details of its Xe3P inference accelerator at Computex
summary: Intel unveiled Crescent Island, a next-gen Data Center GPU with up to 480GB of LPDDR5X memory, at Computex 2026. This inference-optimized chip aims to efficiently handle massive AI contexts. It also features the Xe3P inference accelerator.
insight: Combatting memory shortages in AI applications with massive data contexts.

title: Intel Xeon 6+ Clearwater Forest puts 18A in the data center with up to 288 cores, 576 MB of L3 cache new Xeon 6990E+ is 30% faster per thread than 192-core AMD Epyc 9965, says Intel
summary: Intel's new Xeon 6+ Clearwater Forest CPUs feature up to 288 E-cores and 18A node technology, offering improved performance in data centers. The new Xeon 6990E+ is 30% faster per thread than the 192-core AMD Epyc 9965. This marks Intel's entry into the 18A node market.
insight: Intel gains competitive edge in high-performance data center computing

title: AMD s formerly China-exclusive Radeon RX 9070 GRE goes global for $549 on June 2 RDNA 4 GPU will bridge the gap between RX 9060 XT and RX 9070
summary: AMD launches Radeon RX 9070 GRE globally for $549, bridging performance gap.
insight: Enhanced RDNA 4 technology meets global mid-range graphics demand.

title: Watch Nvidia's Computex 2026 keynote here Jensen Huang takes the stage for Computex and GTC Taipei at 8pm PT / 11pm ET on May 31
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang to deliver keynote address at Computex 2026, potentially revealing the N1X.
insight: Potential unveiling of Nvidia's N1X GPU could impact gaming and AI industries.

title: AMD confirms AM5 support through 2029 Zen 4 and 5 platform will likely see two more generations, at least
summary: AMD extends AM5 socket support to 2029, adding two years to its lifespan. This means the Zen 4 and 5 platforms will likely see two more generations. The decision aims to provide stability for users and developers.
insight: Longer socket support reduces upgrade costs and complexity.

title: AMD brings back Ryzen 7 5800X3D, launches Ryzen 7 7700X3D to combat rising component prices eight-core X3D CPUs arrive under $350 for AM4 or AM5, DDR4 or DDR5
summary: AMD re-releases Ryzen 7 5800X3D and introduces Ryzen 7 7700X3D, both targeting midrange gamers affected by rising component prices with the new chips offering 3D V-Cache technology for improved performance.
insight: Affordable gaming CPUs arrive for price-conscious midrange gamers.

title: Dell XPS 13 targets MacBook Neo with Intel's Wildcat Lake $699 starting price, $599 for students
summary: Dell's XPS 13 targets Apple's MacBook Neo with a lower price, Intel's Wildcat Lake processors, and higher specs.
insight: Dell challenges Apple in the budget laptop market with competitive specs.

title: Alienware debuts 39, 34-inch OLED gaming monitors RGB Stripe Tandem and Penta Tandem tech should boost color performance and text clarity
summary: Alienware launches four new gaming monitors at Computex, featuring OLED and VA panels. The 39 and 34-inch OLED models boast advanced RGB Stripe Tandem and Penta Tandem technologies. These innovations aim to enhance color performance and text clarity.
insight: Boosted color and text clarity for immersive gaming experiences.

title: Nvidia's long-awaited N1/N1X SoC specs leak ahead of Computex launch N1 to feature up to 20 Arm-based cores, standard N1 equipped with 12- and 10-core configs
summary: Nvidia's N1/N1X SoC features various core configurations, including up to 20 Arm-based cores and different CUDA core counts. The N1X has top-end and cut-down options, while the standard N1 offers two configs. This leak precedes the Computex launch of the SoC.
insight: Nvidia's SoC offers versatile performance options for mobile devices

title: SoftBank to spend up to $87 billion on French AI data centers country offers ample nuclear grid that US sites lack
summary: SoftBank plans to spend up to $87 billion on French AI data centers, leveraging the country's nuclear grid to power them. This move comes despite the company carrying significant debt, including a $40 billion loan in March. The investment aims to capitalize on France's abundant energy resources.
insight: France's nuclear grid provides stable energy for AI data centers.

title: New one-meter-cubed 3D printer pumps out large-scale prints at 3kg an hour Modix MAMA-1000 also needs a big wallet with prices starting at $35,000
summary: The Modix MAMA-1000 is a high-throughput 3D printer capable of producing large-scale prints at 3kg per hour. It is designed for industrial use and requires a significant investment. The printer is ideal for companies that need rapid production of complex parts.
insight: High-speed 3D printing boosts industrial manufacturing efficiency and productivity

title: Lenovo Yoga Slim 7x review: Snapdragon X2 Elite makes its case
summary: The Lenovo Yoga Slim 7x offers Snapdragon performance and long battery life, but with limitations such as ARM apps and USB-C only connectivity. It also features an OLED display for an enhanced viewing experience. This laptop is ideal for those who prioritize portability and power.
insight: Snapdragon-powered laptops challenge Intel's dominance in mobile performance

title: Microsoft veteran recalls the last time Nvidia and Arm was the future of Windows shares a video of the first time Windows ran on Nvidia Tegra Arm from 2010
summary: Microsoft veteran Steven Sinofsky recalls the hype around Nvidia and Arm-powered Windows in 2010, referencing the Surface RT as the culmination of this excitement. This era aimed to bring a new PC experience but ultimately faced challenges. The video showcases Windows running on Nvidia Tegra Arm from 2010.
insight: Nvidia and Arm's promise to Windows PC users remains unfulfilled

title: New AI-compute cryptocurrency Pearl sparks a GPU mining rush but profitability is already sliding RTX 5090 daily revenue has halved to $17.19 since April
summary: Pearl cryptocurrency sparks a short-lived GPU mining rush but profitability falls quickly.
insight: Rapid decline in profitability discourages GPU mining investments.

title: The 'ultimate mosquito killer' uses lasers and AI custom model trained to detect and lock lasers on these pests
summary: An expert has created a laser-based mosquito killer using AI and machine learning, which can detect and lock onto mosquitoes with precision.
insight: Eradicating mosquito-borne diseases through high-tech, AI-powered pest control solutions.

title: Orpheus II ISA soundcard returns due to popular demand aimed at DOS and early Windows users, this card includes hardware to support every major audio standard
summary: The Orpheus II ISA soundcard is a retro audio powerhouse, offering support for major audio standards from the era, including Sound Blaster, Gravis UltraSound, OPL3 FM synthesis, and MPU-401 MIDI.
insight: Retro gaming communities will rejoice with authentic audio from their childhood memories.

title: You can still run the original Nvidia Control Panel by grabbing it from the Microsoft Store today app remains useful to adjust a handful of RTX Pro and Quadro features, and may be handy for troubleshooting
summary: The original Nvidia Control Panel is now optional, but still useful, especially for RTX Pro and Quadro features, and for troubleshooting. Users can download it from the Microsoft Store. It offers a handful of adjustments that may not be available in the new version.
insight: Legacy Nvidia Control Panel offers exclusive feature access and troubleshooting tools

title: Bill Gates once starred in a bizarre Doom promo to push Windows 95 back in 1993 tech mogul wore a trench coat, wielded a shotgun, and shot a demon, saying 'Who do you want to execute today?'
summary: Bill Gates starred in a 1995 Windows 95 promo, a Doom-inspired presentation where he wore a trench coat and wielded a shotgun, referencing the popular game Doom.
insight: Microsoft used gaming to promote Windows 95's capabilities in 1995.

title: Sixfab AI HAT+ for Raspberry Pi 5 integrates DEEPX DX-M1 AI accelerator
summary: Sixfab has released the AI HAT+ for Raspberry Pi 5, integrating the DEEPX DX-M1 AI accelerator. The board is available in two versions with 13 TOPS and 25 TOPS performance. It enables local vision AI tasks on the Pi 5.
insight: Accelerates local vision AI tasks on Raspberry Pi 5 efficiently

title: Nuvoton NuMaker-GestureAI-M55M1 module combines Cortex-M55 MCU with GC0308 camera for AI gesture control
summary: Nuvoton has released the NuMaker-GestureAI-M55M1 development module for AI vision applications, combining the NuMicro M55M1 MCU with a GC0308 camera for gesture control.
insight: AI-powered gesture control for touchless interfaces and vision systems is emerging.

title: Waveshare RP2350B-Plus-W A Raspberry Pi Pico 2 W-sized board with 41 GPIOs, 16MB flash, USB-C port
summary: The Waveshare RP2350B-Plus-W is a Raspberry Pi Pico 2 W-sized board with 41 GPIOs, 16MB flash, and a USB-C port, providing an upgrade over the official board with additional GPIOs on the bottom.
insight: More GPIOs and USB-C port offer enhanced development possibilities.

title: Radxa s 2026 Qualcomm hardware: Dragon Q8B and Q5E SBCs, DragonStation and DragonBay NAS systems
summary: Radxa showcased new Qualcomm-based SBCs and NAS systems at a developer day, including the Q8B SBC with a Snapdragon 8cx Gen3 SoC and the Q5E SBC with a Dragonwing QCS6690 SoC, as part of a 2026 roadmap featuring 22 Qualcomm systems.
insight: Radxa expands Qualcomm-based product lineup with high-performance SBCs and NAS systems.

title: Qualcomm unveils robot reference design, IQ10
summary: Qualcomm unveiled the IQ10 robot reference design at Computex in Taiwan, led by CEO Cristiano Amon. This design is intended to accelerate the development of robots and related technologies. It showcases Qualcomm's efforts in expanding its presence in the robotics industry.
insight: Qualcomm's IQ10 accelerates robotics innovation and adoption rapidly

title: Humanoids are insanely hard to build, but Nvidia has a plan
summary: Nvidia and Unitree H2 Plus announced a humanoid robot reference design at GTC Taipei, aiming to simplify the development of humanoid robots. The collaboration combines Unitree's robotics expertise with Nvidia's AI and computing technology. This partnership has the potential to accelerate humanoid robotics advancements.
insight: Nvidia's plan simplifies humanoid robot development with AI and computing technology

title: Intel intros OpenVINO for robots; 130 firms use Ultra Series 3
summary: Intel introduces OpenVINO for robots at Computex with Ella, a robot barrista. The upgrade to Series 3 reduces software complexity and increases ROI. The Ultra Series 3 is used by 130 companies.
insight: Simplifying AI processing boosts efficiency and profit for robotics companies.

title: Intel tackles tracking of data center energy use with AET
summary: Intel is using Application Energy Telemetry (AET) to better track data center energy use, aiming to improve Total Cost of Ownership (TCO). AET allows for more accurate monitoring and management of data center energy consumption. This will help Intel optimize energy efficiency and reduce costs.
insight: Accurate energy monitoring optimizes data center TCO and efficiency.

title: Engineer builds AI laser defense system that wiped out every mosquito in his home
summary: An engineer built an AI laser defense system that completely eliminated mosquitoes from his home using a custom-trained visual model and a high-magnification camera setup.
insight: AI can efficiently solve complex real-world problems with precision and accuracy.

title: MSI unveils the MEG Vision X2 AI+ gaming PC with LuckyClaw AI agent and holographic Holostage companion
summary: MSI introduces the MEG Vision X2 AI+ gaming PC with LuckyClaw AI agent and holographic Holostage companion. The AI companion responds to natural speech commands for seamless, hands-free control. It controls performance profiles, monitor settings, RGB lighting, and more.
insight: AI-powered gaming experiences become more immersive and user-friendly suddenly

title: United flight turns around after passenger's Bluetooth speaker name triggers security scare
summary: United flight 236 turned back after a passenger's Bluetooth speaker name raised security concerns.
insight: Innocuous items can inadvertently trigger security protocols and alter flight courses.

title: DLSS 4.5 Ray Reconstruction arrives this August to make ray tracing look cleaner and more stable
summary: DLSS 4.5 Ray Reconstruction is set to arrive in August, replacing traditional denoisers with an AI-driven approach to improve ray tracing quality and stability.
insight: AI-driven denoising enhances ray tracing for cleaner and more stable visuals.

title: Mathematicians used Minecraft mobs to calculate Pi without writing a single line of code
summary: Mathematicians used Minecraft mobs to calculate Pi without writing code, leveraging probability and game mechanics.
insight: Rethinking traditional problem-solving approaches can lead to innovative solutions.

title: AMD at Computex: Ryzen 7 5800X3D revival, 7700X3D launch, RX 9070 GRE goes global, and AM5 support through 2029
summary: AMD announces the revival of Ryzen 7 5800X3D, launch of 7700X3D, and RX 9070 GRE.
insight: AMD expands options for gamers and content creators with new product lineup.

title: Nvidia RTX Spark CPU is now official: "superchip" will power Windows laptops and desktops
summary: Nvidia has officially announced the RTX Spark CPU, a 'superchip' for Windows laptops and desktops, marking a significant step in the development of advanced computing hardware.
insight: Nvidia's RTX Spark CPU boosts performance in Windows devices significantly.

title: Moore's law is hitting a wall, so researchers are stacking silicon chip layers instead of shrinking them
summary: Researchers are stacking silicon chip layers to bypass shrinking limits, enabling more complex designs.
insight: Breaking the 2D paradigm with 3D stacked silicon chip architecture.

title: A Trump-linked startup is sending humanoid robots to the Ukraine battlefield
summary: Foundation Future Industries, a Trump-linked startup, is sending humanoid robots to Ukraine. The goal is to create machines for challenging, dangerous human roles. This move aims to reduce human casualties in conflict zones.
insight: Autonomous robots could minimize human losses in war-torn areas.

title: MIT researchers found a way to get lithium out of hard rock without the massive energy bill
summary: MIT researchers have discovered a method to extract lithium from hard rock more efficiently, potentially disrupting the lithium-ion battery market by reducing costs.
insight: Revolutionizing lithium extraction to make alternative battery technologies cost-competitive.

title: California passes bill requiring gun-blocking software in 3D printers
summary: California passes bill requiring 3D printers to include gun-blocking software. The bill aims to prevent 3D printed firearms by checking design files before a print job begins. This technology will be mandatory for consumer 3D printers sold in California.
insight: Preventing 3D printed firearms reduces gun-related violence risk.

title: Fake downloads of popular PC utilities are quietly installing crypto miners on enthusiast PCs
summary: Scammers are manipulating search engine results to install crypto miners on PCs with dedicated GPUs, targeting users with AI chatbot responses.
insight: Vulnerable PC enthusiasts are being hijacked for cryptocurrency mining operations.
